audit_items.proof_trace: At line 982, `passwd.bv_val = ber_strdup(optarg)` is called without a NULL check; `ber_strdup` returns NULL when `ber_memalloc` fails (OOM). The result is immediately passed to `strlen(passwd.bv_val)` at line 990, causing a NULL dereference (SIGSEGV). No guard exists on any code path between the allocation and the `strlen` call.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker who can induce memory exhaustion on the host (e.g., via ulimit manipulation or OS-level memory pressure) and cause a victim to run an LDAP client tool with `-w password` would trigger the NULL dereference crash, denying service to the client process.
